APIActionRowComponent<APIComponentInMess.description: An Action Row is a top-level layout component used in messages. Use in modals is deprecated.

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

parse.description: An array of allowed mention types to parse from the content

replied_user.description: For replies, whether to mention the author of the message being replied to

roles.description: Array of role_ids to mention (Max size of 100)

users.description: Array of user_ids to mention (Max size of 100)

custom_id.description: A developer-defined identifier for the select menu, max 100 characters

default_values.description: List of default values for auto-populated select menu components

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

max_values.description: The maximum number of items that can be chosen; max 25

min_values.description: The minimum number of items that must be chosen; min 0, max 25

placeholder.description: Custom placeholder text if nothing is selected, max 150 characters

required.description: Whether the component is required to answer in a modal.

APIButtonComponent.description:
A Button is an interactive component that can only be used in messages. It creates clickable elements that users can interact with, sending an interaction to your app when clicked.

Buttons must be placed inside an Action Row or a Section's accessory field.

custom_id.description: The custom_id to be sent in the interaction when clicked

emoji.description: The emoji to display to the left of the text

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

label.description: The label to be displayed on the button

anyOf.description: The most important or recommended action in a group of options

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

sku_id.description: The id for a purchasable SKU

emoji.description: The emoji to display to the left of the text

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

label.description: The label to be displayed on the button

url.description: The URL to direct users to when clicked for Link buttons

APIChannelSelectComponent.description:
A Channel Select is an interactive component that allows users to select one or more channels in a message. Options are automatically populated based on available channels in the server and can be filtered by channel types.

Channel Selects can be configured for both single-select and multi-select behavior. When a user finishes making their choice(s) your app receives an interaction.

Channel Selects must be placed inside an Action Row and are only available in messages. An Action Row can contain only one select menu and cannot contain buttons if it has a select menu.

channel_types.description: List of channel types to include in the ChannelSelect component

custom_id.description: A developer-defined identifier for the select menu, max 100 characters

default_values.description: List of default values for auto-populated select menu components

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

max_values.description: The maximum number of items that can be chosen; max 25

min_values.description: The minimum number of items that must be chosen; min 0, max 25

placeholder.description: Custom placeholder text if nothing is selected, max 150 characters

required.description: Whether the component is required to answer in a modal.

APIContainerComponent.description:
A Container is a top-level layout component that holds up to 10 components. Containers are visually distinct from surrounding components and has an optional customizable color bar.

Containers are only available in messages.

accent_color.description: Color for the accent on the container as RGB from `0x000000` to `0xFFFFFF`

components.description: Up to 10 components of the type action row, text display, section, media gallery, separator, or file

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

spoiler.description: Whether the container should be a spoiler (or blurred out)

description.description:
Description of embed

Length limit: 4096 characters

fields.description:
Fields information

Length limit: 25 field objects

flags.description: Embed flags combined as a bitfield

title.description:
Title of embed

Length limit: 256 characters

type.description: Type of embed (always "rich" for webhook embeds)

icon_url.description: URL of author icon (only supports http(s) and attachments)

name.description:
Name of author

Length limit: 256 characters

proxy_icon_url.description: A proxied url of author icon

inline.description: Whether or not this field should display inline

name.description:
Name of the field

Length limit: 256 characters

value.description:
Value of the field

Length limit: 1024 characters

icon_url.description: URL of footer icon (only supports http(s) and attachments)

proxy_icon_url.description: A proxied url of footer icon

flags.description: Embed media flags combined as a bitfield

proxy_url.description: A proxied url of the image

url.description: Source url of image (only supports http(s) and attachments)

flags.description: Embed media flags combined as a bitfield

proxy_url.description: A proxied url of the video

APIFileComponent.description:
A File is a top-level component that allows you to display an uploaded file as an attachment to the message and reference it in the component.

Each file component can only display 1 attached file, but you can upload multiple files and add them to different file components within your payload.

Files are only available in messages.

file.description: This unfurled media item is unique in that it **only** support attachment references using the `attachment://<filename>` syntax

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

name.description: The name of the file. This field is ignored and provided by the API as part of the response

size.description: The size of the file in bytes. This field is ignored and provided by the API as part of the response

spoiler.description: Whether the media should be a spoiler (or blurred out)

APIMediaGalleryComponent.description:
A Media Gallery is a top-level content component that allows you to display 1-10 media attachments in an organized gallery format. Each item can have optional descriptions and can be marked as spoilers.

Media Galleries are only available in messages.

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

spoiler.description: Whether the media should be a spoiler (or blurred out)

APIMentionableSelectComponent.description:
A Mentionable Select is an interactive component that allows users to select one or more mentionables in a message. Options are automatically populated based on available mentionables in the server.

Mentionable Selects can be configured for both single-select and multi-select behavior. When a user finishes making their choice(s), your app receives an interaction.

Mentionable Selects must be placed inside an Action Row and are only available in messages. An Action Row can contain only one select menu and cannot contain buttons if it has a select menu.

name.description: Emoji name (can be null only in reaction emoji objects)

text.description:
The text of the field

The maximum length is `300` for the question, and `55` for any answer

APIRoleSelectComponent.description:
A Role Select is an interactive component that allows users to select one or more roles in a message. Options are automatically populated based on the server's available roles.

Role Selects can be configured for both single-select and multi-select behavior. When a user finishes making their choice(s) your app receives an interaction.

Role Selects must be placed inside an Action Row and are only available in messages. An Action Row can contain only one select menu and cannot contain buttons if it has a select menu.

APISectionComponent.description:
A Section is a top-level layout component that allows you to join text contextually with an accessory.

Sections are only available in messages.

accessory.description: A thumbnail or a button component, with a future possibility of adding more compatible components

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

default.description: Whether this option should be already-selected by default

description.description: An additional description of the option (max 100 chars)

emoji.description: The emoji to display to the left of the option

label.description: The user-facing name of the option (max 100 chars)

value.description: The dev-defined value of the option (max 100 chars)

APISeparatorComponent.description:
A Separator is a top-level layout component that adds vertical padding and visual division between other components.

Separators are only available in messages.

divider.description: Whether a visual divider should be displayed in the component

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

APIStringSelectComponent.description:
A String Select is an interactive component that allows users to select one or more provided options in a message.

String Selects can be configured for both single-select and multi-select behavior. When a user finishes making their choice(s) your app receives an interaction.

An Action Row can contain only one select menu and cannot contain buttons if it has a select menu.

custom_id.description: A developer-defined identifier for the select menu, max 100 characters

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

max_values.description: The maximum number of items that can be chosen; max 25

min_values.description: The minimum number of items that must be chosen; min 0, max 25

options.description: Specified choices in a select menu; max 25

placeholder.description: Custom placeholder text if nothing is selected, max 150 characters

required.description: Whether the component is required to answer in a modal.

APITextDisplayComponent.description:
A Text Display is a top-level content component that allows you to add text to your message formatted with markdown and mention users and roles. This is similar to the content field of a message, but allows you to add multiple text components, controlling the layout of your message.

Text Displays are only available in messages.

content.description: Text that will be displayed similar to a message

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

APIThumbnailComponent.description:
A Thumbnail is a content component that is a small image only usable as an accessory in a section. The preview comes from an url or attachment through the unfurled media item structure.

Thumbnails are only available in messages as an accessory in a section.

id.description:
32 bit integer used as an optional identifier for component

The id field is optional and is used to identify components in the response from an interaction that aren't interactive components. The id must be unique within the message and is generated sequentially if left empty. Generation of ids won't use another id that exists in the message if you have one defined for another component.

spoiler.description: Whether the thumbnail should be a spoiler (or blurred out)

attachment_id.description: The id of the uploaded attachment.

content_type.description: The media type of the content

flags.description: Unfurled media item flags combined as a bitfield

height.description: The height of the media item (if image or video)

placeholder_version.description: Version of the placeholder (if image or video)

proxy_url.description: The proxied url of the media item

width.description: The width of the media item (if image or video)

APIUserSelectComponent.description:
A User Select is an interactive component that allows users to select one or more users in a message. Options are automatically populated based on the server's available users.

User Selects can be configured for both single-select and multi-select behavior. When a user finishes making their choice(s) your app receives an interaction.

User Selects must be placed inside an Action Row and are only available in messages. An Action Row can contain only one select menu and cannot contain buttons if it has a select menu.

description.description: Description (alt text) for the file (max 1024 characters)

duration_secs.description: The duration of the audio file (currently for voice messages)

id.description: Attachment id or a number that matches `n` in `files[n]`

title.description:
The original filename of the upload with special characters preserved

This will be present when the filename contains special characters (e.g. Cyrillic), in which case the `filename` field will be a sanitized version without those characters

waveform.description: Base64 encoded bytearray representing a sampled waveform (currently for voice messages)

allow_multiselect.description: Whether a user can select multiple answers

answers.description: Each of the answers available in the poll, up to 10

duration.description: Number of hours the poll should be open for, up to 32 days

layout_type.description: The layout type of the poll

applied_tags.description: Array of tag ids to apply to the thread

attachments.description: Attachment objects with filename and description

avatar_url.description: Override the default avatar of the webhook

components.description:
The components to include with the message

Application-owned webhooks can always send components. Non-application-owned webhooks cannot send interactive components, and the `components` field will be ignored unless they set the `with_components` query param.

flags.description: Message flags combined as a bitfield

thread_name.description:
Name of thread to create

Available only if the webhook is in a forum channel and a thread is not specified in  {@link  RESTPostAPIWebhookWithTokenQuery.thread_id }  query parameter

tts.description: `true` if this is a TTS message

username.description: Override the default username of the webhook
